item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay! Also, I’m 78% sure. Might wanna double check.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-5339 said, "Mommy, the water's too deep".

Shit. This article gave me shivers in my back, that fucking quote. Are you sure you aren't Trevor Henderson?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s very kind of you! Thank you very much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gave me very similar vibes to SCP-5031, and considering how much I enjoy that article, that is a big compliment to your story. Great job! I've got an audio adaptation in the works. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd almost think that the child drowned but the hand lived… except I don't understand how the hand was already waiting at the home once they returned from the vacation.

Kind of scratching my head second-guessing whether or not I truly understood the implications presented here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seen this come a long way and I was excited to read it once I had seen it came out!

You did a really good job and I’m looking forward to anything new you have in the works for the future.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very interesting to see how far this one came, and the finished product turned out great. Goes on for just the right length.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah shoot, I've only spotted that now. Sorry. Yeah, the date is enough. I skimmed past that without knowing, heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HA! No, I really like his work though and he inspired me to make my own!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: really made me think this was trevor henderson huh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m so glad to see this up! You did a great job with it and I feel privileged to have seen it in the draft stage :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved this from the start and I love it now! I think you already know I am gonna give this a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your crit! I changed your nitpicks so they fit and stuff. On a side note; the English lessons are taking place over two years. Babies can't start to speak after 16 months I believe so I wanted to make that realistic. Is there a way I could make this more clear besides the date on the top of the test log?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh.

Oh fuck.

Now I'm sad.

+1 you brilliant person.
After seeing you develop this for so long, I'm glad to see it come to fruition. Congrats, my guy. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ some general stuff
- hide

I dig the ending; it's dark, and has a bit of the "WTF" moment. The escalation was kinda there, though it felt a tad disconnected between the last part of the second test log and the final reveal? I was a bit uncertain whether 5339 would actually react a little differently/strangely to the picture of the ocean instead of merely reciting that it's "deep water".

Also, I'm really not sure about how a one-year old talks and behaves as they learn new words, but the "Mommy, the water's too deep" bit felt… a little too complete and sounded more like at least a 5 year old instead of a one-year old? Idk. It's fine to leave it unchanged if a one-year old is totally capable of saying that, but the previous lessons where it could recite like two words at maximum felt… a little off?

he expired due to unknown circumstances, while on vacation with Mrs. Ideme.

I am a little unsure if Mrs. Ideme, like, left Mickey where she drowned him and came back home to act as if Mickey was gone? Because if that is the case, and that the Foundation explicitly told us that he died by merely saying "expired", but they just don't know how he died, I find it a little difficult to believe that the Foundation wouldn't like, perform an autopsy to find his lungs like, flooded with water and then make the connection to him drowning?

+ some very nitpicky stuff
- hide

ASL lessons by a rotation of researchers who SCP-5339 appears to have an emotional connection to.

I think it could be me, but this reads a little weird? I'm not sure how to restructure this, but I think it would be:

…ASL lessons on a rotational shift basis by selected1 researchers.

spaced equidistantly to stabilize it.

Hm. I'm thinking "to stabilize it" feels… a little unneeded?

a variety of tests that may result in a response by SCP-5339 are to be conducted.

I think cutting "that may result… are to be" and placing "were" after "tests" could flow a little better?

weekly English lessons are to be implemented into SCP-5339's containment procedures.

Maybe cut "to be"?

Footnotes
1. A prerequisite for selection is that SCP-5339 appears to have a positive emotional connection to the researcher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god….I didn't really understand what was going on until the end there…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Photo by me!

For those interested in my work, you can find more of it here —> https://www.instagram.com/supposedly_spooky/
